name: Suriname
avoid: May–July is the long rainy season, when Brownsberg roads turn muddy, waterfalls run harder, and interior flight schedules get less predictable.
Interior charters use strict soft-bag baggage limits; pack light before any Gum Air sector to Raleighvallen or other bush airstrips.
Cards work in larger Paramaribo hotels, but cash is still important for taxis, buses, small ferries, village stops, and park entrances outside the capital.
stopoverNote: Most India trips reach Paramaribo via Amsterdam or the Caribbean; keep Paramaribo at both ends so you can handle cash, SIM, and any Gum Air or river-lodge logistics more easily.

cities.name: Paramaribo
cities.notes: UNESCO-listed capital base for Waterkant evenings, Fort Zeelandia, multicultural food, and easy links onward by taxi, bus, or bush-flight transfer.

cities.name: Brownsberg Nature Park
cities.notes: A classic dry-season rainforest escape for lake panoramas, monkey sightings, and half-day hikes to Leo and Irene Falls above Brokopondo Reservoir.

cities.name: Central Suriname Nature Reserve
cities.notes: Plan this as a lodge-style wilderness segment centered on Raleighvallen, Foengoe Island, and the Voltzberg trail, usually reached by Gum Air charter plus boat.

cities.name: Commewijne
cities.notes: River district east of Paramaribo known for old plantations, dolphin-spotting boat trips, Peperpot walks, and relaxed heritage stays such as Frederiksdorp.

itinerary.sim: Telesur and Digicel prepaid data usually lands around ₹1,200–₹2,000 for enough maps, WhatsApp, and lodge coordination; sort it out in Paramaribo before heading inland.

Paramaribo.name: Paramaribo
Paramaribo.note: Stay near the Torarica riverfront, Zeelandiafort area, or a central lodge so you can walk the old town in the cooler hours and keep airport or charter transfers easy.

days.theme: Waterkant heritage, Fort Zeelandia, and a first taste of Suriname

activities.name: Waterkant, Independence Square, Presidential Palace garden side, and Palmentuin walk
Fort Zeelandia museum
activities.tip: Standard entry is commonly listed around SRD25, which converts to roughly ₹58.

activities.name: St. Peter and Paul Basilica and Keizerstraat mosque-synagogue exterior circuit
Dinner at Garden of Eden or Zus & Zo

days.theme: Markets, riverside culture, and an easy city day before the interior

activities.name: Paramaribo Central Market and Waterkant produce stalls
Surinaams Museum
Taxi hops between the old town, Hermitage Mall side, and hotel zone
Evening roti, pom, or peanut soup crawl

Brownsberg Nature Park.name: Brownsberg Nature Park
Brownsberg Nature Park.note: This works best as an early-start overnight or full-day nature outing from Paramaribo or Brokopondo, especially in the drier windows when trails stay safer and the park road is easier.

days.theme: Brokopondo panorama, waterfall trails, and Suriname's easiest rainforest hit

activities.name: STINASU Brownsberg Nature Park entrance
activities.tip: Adult admission is commonly listed around SRD35, which converts to roughly ₹81.

activities.name: Mazaroni Top viewpoint and Brokopondo Reservoir lookouts
Leo or Irene Falls hike with local guide tip
Taxi or private transfer from Paramaribo/Brokopondo

Central Suriname Nature Reserve.name: Central Suriname Nature Reserve
Central Suriname Nature Reserve.note: Treat this as a packaged wilderness leg: most travelers fly from Zorg en Hoop to Raleighvallen, then continue by river boat to Foengoe Island or the Voltzberg base with meals and guiding bundled.

days.theme: Gum Air bush flight, Coppename River, and remote-lodge arrival

activities.name: Gum Air charter from Zorg en Hoop to Raleighvallen airstrip
activities.tip: Interior charters to Raleighvallen are commonly quoted around US$350 one way, roughly ₹29,000, before any lodge packaging.

activities.name: River boat transfer to Foengoe Island camp or lodge base
Coppename River sunset and wildlife spotting around Raleighvallen

days.theme: Voltzberg climb, river life, and full immersion in the reserve

activities.name: Voltzberg summit hike with ranger/guide
activities.cost: Usually bundled in ₹58,000–₹82,000 pp multi-day reserve packages

activities.name: Raleigh Falls and Foengoe Island canoe or boat outing
activities.cost: Included or ₹2,300–₹4,600 pp as an add-on

activities.name: Birdwatching for macaws, toucans, and monkeys around camp

Commewijne.name: Commewijne
Commewijne.note: Do this as a day trip or one-night wind-down from Paramaribo, focusing on Peperpot, Nieuw Amsterdam, or Frederiksdorp rather than trying to cram every plantation into one loop.

days.theme: Plantation stories, river boats, dolphins, and a quieter side of the capital region

activities.name: Commewijne plantation boat tour covering Frederiksdorp, Rust en Werk, and river dolphins
activities.tip: Current operator pricing is commonly around €67–€75 per person, which works out to roughly ₹6.3k–₹7.1k.

activities.name: Peperpot Nature Park walk or cycle loop
Mariënburg plantation ruins photo stop
Bus or taxi from Paramaribo to Meerzorg / Commewijne side

connections.method: Shared taxi, private driver, or bus toward Brownsweg plus onward arranged transfer (about 2.5–3.5 h depending on road conditions)
connections.cost: ₹350–₹700 pp by bus plus transfer / ₹3,500–₹6,500 per car by taxi

connections.method: Return to Paramaribo, then Gum Air bush flight from Zorg en Hoop to Raleighvallen and continue by river boat to the reserve lodges
connections.cost: ₹31,500–₹39,000 pp including the flight and typical river transfer

connections.method: Gum Air back to Paramaribo, then taxi or public bus east across the Jules Wijdenbosch Bridge to Meerzorg, Peperpot, or Frederiksdorp
connections.cost: ₹29,500–₹33,000 pp including the return flight sector and road transfer

extras: Line buses and minibuses are the cheapest way to reach Brownsweg or Meerzorg, but schedules are informal and luggage space is limited, so most travelers still use taxis for early departures.
Gum Air interior sectors normally require soft bags and light packing; confirm your lodge's baggage cap before leaving heavy suitcases at the Paramaribo hotel.
If you only have five or six days, cut Brownsberg or Commewijne first and protect the Paramaribo buffer days around your interior flight.
Dry months from February to April and August to November are the safest windows for combining city walks, Brownsberg hikes, and river-based reserve outings in one trip.
Restaurant quality is strongest in Paramaribo; once you move into Brownsberg or the reserve, expect set menus, simple kitchens, and much earlier nights.

Paramaribo: Saint Peter and Paul Basilica Paramaribo
